pool.baseline: idk man. i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from james webb and it just hit me. everything we do, every single thing... it's all just noise. the universe is so massive and indifferent, and in a few billion years the sun dies and everything just goes dark. what is even the point of trying to build anything or be anything if none of it actually matters in the long run? it feels like we're all just screaming into a void that doesn't even hear us.

pool.baseline: i honestly dont even know why im still waking up to do this. the lab results came back today and it’s just... it’s over. three years of work, all that funding, and it’s just gone. i feel like there’s nothing left of me outside of this research. like i’ve just spent my whole life building something that doesn't exist anymore. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers its last, much like the light in my own soul. He is gone... the vows he swore were nothing but ash on the wind. I look at this world and see only a cold, grey void where my heart used to beat. There is no more poetry left in my life, only the heavy silence of a broken thing. It would be so much more graceful to simply let the darkness take me, to drift away into the night and never wake to this wretched sun again.
